serving.natural_language_portion: One whole pizza of approximately 300 g, as typically served at a restaurant, representing a single-person portion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat topping (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite); nduja nutrient profile estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for Italian pork sausage and published nduja composition data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for nduja pizza with hot honey exists in any approved primary source. Values estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for: (1) cheese pizza (FDC ID 170919, SR Legacy), (2) Italian pork sausage (FDC ID 167932, SR Legacy) as the closest approved proxy for nduja, and (3) honey (FDC ID 169640, SR Legacy) for the hot honey component. Bell peppers and cherry tomatoes contribute minor macro adjustments. Per-100g values represent a weighted composite of the assembled pizza. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Pizza, cheese, thin crust (GI ~50, reference glucose).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for nduja pizza with hot honey. GI of 50 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza (thin crust), which is the closest approved analogous food. The nduja topping and hot honey add fat, protein, and a small amount of sugar; the overall GI is not expected to differ materially from a standard cheese pizza. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 76.8) / 100 = 38.4, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 25.6 × 3 = 76.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~580 mg per 100 g; ~1740 mg per full pizza serving) from nduja, cheese, and pizza dough
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load per serving, which can substantially exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg in a single meal; if consumed, portion size should be strictly limited to one or two slices.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~38 per full pizza serving) driven by refined wheat dough, partially offset by fat and protein from nduja and fior di latte
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this pizza due to its high total glycemic load per serving; consuming a smaller portion (one to two slices) alongside non-starchy vegetables and monitoring post-meal blood glucose is advisable, as the fat and protein content from nduja and cheese will slow but not eliminate the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.9 g per 100 g; ~14.7 g per full serving) and high sodium from nduja and fior di latte
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This pizza is high in both saturated fat and sodium, two key dietary risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with existing CVD or elevated LDL cholesterol should limit consumption to occasional small portions and consider requesting reduced cheese or nduja quantities.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Pork Nduja Pizza with Hot Honey is an Italian-style pizza originating from Calabrian culinary tradition, served at Mingle restaurant in Funan, Singapore. The pizza is topped with fior di latte (fresh, milky mozzarella), nduja (a fiery, spreadable Calabrian pork sausage made from pork fat, offal, and Calabrian chilli peppers), roasted or fresh bell peppers, cherry tomatoes, and finished with a drizzle of hot honey — a condiment combining honey with chilli heat. The crust is typically thin and baked at high temperature in a wood-fired or deck oven, producing a crisp base with a slightly charred edge. The flavour profile is intensely savoury, spicy, and subtly sweet from the hot honey, with the creamy fior di latte balancing the heat of the nduja. Nutritionally, this pizza is energy-dense (approximately 248 kcal per 100 g), with meaningful protein from the pork and cheese, significant saturated fat from the nduja and dairy, and a moderate-to-high carbohydrate load from the dough. Sodium content is high due to the cured pork sausage and cheese. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low GI) based on cheese pizza analogues, but the glycemic load per full serving is substantial (~38) due to portion size. Clinically, this dish is of concern for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium per serving), cardiovascular disease (high saturated fat and sodium), and Type 2 Diabetes (high total glycemic load per serving). It is not suitable for halal, kosher, or vegetarian diets. The dish is priced at SGD 34.50 at Mingle, Funan, Singapore.
common_user_queries: Is spicy pork nduja pizza healthy?
How many calories in nduja pizza?
Is nduja pizza high in sodium?
What is hot honey on pizza?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for nduja pizza with hot honey exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite data for cheese pizza (SR Legacy), Italian pork sausage as nduja proxy, and honey. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous food (cheese pizza, thin crust) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite used for this estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA source composite for this dish. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite for pizza with meat topping. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, folate, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and water values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and should be treated as approximate. The food_name has been expanded to 'Spicy Pork Nduja Pizza with Hot Honey' to reflect the canonical dish type (pizza) more precisely, consistent with the description provided in the input record. The original input food_name 'Spicy Pork Nduja With Hot Honey' is preserved in aliases. This is an Italian cuisine dish; multilingual naming requirements apply to Singaporean dishes only — Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are not applicable for this Italian dish and are correctly set to null. The romanized Italian name 'Pizza con Nduja Piccante e Miele Piccante' is provided as a romanized_local alias for completeness.

improvement_actions_taken: Expanded food_name from 'Spicy Pork Nduja With Hot Honey' to 'Spicy Pork Nduja Pizza with Hot Honey' to reflect canonical dish type.

Calculated GL per serving as (50 × 76.8) / 100 = 38.4 for 300 g serving.
